name: Pico de Boumort - Circular Route — Sierra de Boumort
name_original: Pico de Boumort - Ruta Circular — Sierra de Boumort
region_intro: Hiking around Abella De La Conca offers diverse terrain within a dramatic mountain environment, characterized by massive limestone fins and spectacular rock formations. The region is situated at the foot of the Sierra del Carreu and Boumort, featuring extensive areas for exploration. Hikers can discover river valleys, forested trails, and ancient olive groves. This landscape provides varied opportunities for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Abella De La Conca The most popular hiking route is Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt — circular through the Bosque de Sant Corneli, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes about 6 hours to complete. This difficult route explores forested areas and offers a circular experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico de Boumort - Circular Route — Sierra de Boumort, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.5 km) path. This circular trail ascends to Pico de Boumort, offering views of the Sierra de Boumort. Local hikers also love the Sant Corneli Loop, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail leading through varied local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Hiking around Abella De La Conca is defined by dramatic mountain environments, limestone formations, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Abella De La Conca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Abella De La Conca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Abella de la Conca?
A: Abella de la Conca offers a diverse network of around 16 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging ascents through its dramatic mountain environment.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Abella de la Conca?
A: Hiking in Abella de la Conca reveals stunning natural features, including massive limestone fins and spectacular rock formations. You can explore river valleys, ancient olive groves, and forested trails lined with pine trees. Notable features include the unique tufa-stuffed rock arches of the Forat dels Lladres and swirling rock pools in the river valleys.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Abella de la Conca?
A: Yes, Abella de la Conca has several circular routes. For an easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219793097/sant-corneli-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Corneli Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.9 km. A moderate circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925908170/pico-de-boumort-circular-route-sierra-de-boumort" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Boumort - Circular Route — Sierra de Boumort&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the Sierra de Boumort. For a more challenging circular experience, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220076476/sant-pere-de-vilanoveta-from-aramunt-circular-through-the-bosque-de-sant-corneli" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Vilanoveta from Aramunt&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Abella de la Conca?
A: The region offers beautiful scenery year-round. February and March are particularly scenic when the almond groves are in bloom. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for hiking. While trails lead to views of snowy Pyrenees peaks in the distance, specific winter conditions can vary, so it's always best to check local weather before heading out.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Abella de la Conca?
A: Yes, there are easier routes suitable for families. The unique geography allows for diverse walking experiences, with options ranging from gentle strolls through olive groves to more accessible paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219793097/sant-corneli-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Corneli Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, shorter option that could be suitable for families.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls or rock pools along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, hikers can discover cooling rock pools in the river valleys below the village. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the area, offering a beautiful natural spectacle.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while hiking in the area?
A: Beyond natural beauty, you can explore the medieval village of Abella de la Conca itself, visit the Abella Castle, the ancient church of Sant Esteve, and the Romanesque bridge of Bóixols. The secluded hermitage of Carranima is also reachable by trail and offers panoramic views.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about Abella de la Conca?
A: The hiking routes in Abella de la Conca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 18 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic mountain environments, the unique limestone formations, and the tranquil river valleys, highlighting the diverse terrain and stunning views.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery in Abella de la Conca?
A: Absolutely. Trails lead to stunning lookout points, such as Col del Trumfo at 1462m, which offers spectacular views over nearby lakes and the snowy peaks of the Pyrenees in the distance. On clear days, the hermitage of Carranima provides views all the way to Montserrat.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Abella de la Conca?
A: The remote and protected location of Abella de la Conca supports a rich array of wildlife. You might spot abundant Griffon vultures, black vultures, Egyptian vultures, and other birds of prey. The area is also home to over 40 species of butterflies, indicating a healthy and diverse ecosystem.

Q: Are there any caves or gorges to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region features interesting geological formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3231895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de la Serpent&lt;/a&gt; is a cave highlight in the area. For a more extensive experience, the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt; offers dramatic scenery and further exploration opportunities.
tips: Small stone shelter. It is located in the Sierra de Boumort, near the homonymous peak. It's basically a survival shelter.
The Pico de Boumort is the highest peak of the Sierra de Boumort at 2077.2 m altitude.
